day_beats.story_beat: A kid stops in front of the shelf, mouth open. 'They still make these?' His older sister steers him toward the food court. He looks back twice.
day_beats.forward_hook: Word travels fast. A collector mentioned she'd swing by tomorrow with a specific wish list.

day_beats.story_beat: A woman in a blazer picks up a cartridge, turns it over, sets it back down. She doesn't buy — but she lingers longer than most.
day_beats.forward_hook: The weekend crowd tends to run younger. Keep the counter clear and the prices honest.

day_beats.story_beat: Someone left a handwritten note in the returns bin: 'This game saved my summer. Hope it saves someone else's too.' No name.
day_beats.forward_hook: Rumor from the food court: a reseller is doing the rounds. Price accordingly.

day_beats.story_beat: The mall's overhead PA plays a jingle that hasn't aired since 1998. Three customers stop mid-sentence and just listen.
day_beats.forward_hook: You're starting to see regulars. That's a good sign — it means they trust the stock.

day_beats.story_beat: A teenager haggles for twenty minutes on a ten-dollar game, then buys it at full price anyway. He seems satisfied regardless.
day_beats.forward_hook: The weekend is close. Fridays move more cartridges than any other day of the week.

day_beats.story_beat: Two customers arrive separately, both looking for the same item. One leaves with it. The other takes your number. You didn't know you had a number until you gave it.
day_beats.forward_hook: End of your first week tomorrow. Take stock of what sold and what didn't. The shelf tells a story if you let it.

day_beats.story_beat: A little girl asks if you work here every day. You say yes. She nods like this is important information she will remember. Maybe she will.
day_beats.forward_hook: Week two. The patterns are becoming visible. Stock what moves; rotate what doesn't.

fallback_beat: The mall hums along. Another day logged.
fallback_hook: Tomorrow brings fresh inventory and new faces.

midday_events.title: Overflow Stock Offer
midday_events.body: A vendor from two doors down knocks. He's got a box of unclaimed trade-ins he can't move. Five dollars for the lot, untested.

midday_events.title: Kid With No Manners
midday_events.body: A twelve-year-old is handling every cartridge in the bargain bin. He's putting them back wrong. Do you say something?

midday_events.title: Power Flicker
midday_events.body: The lights brown out for two seconds. Display TVs restart. Customers barely notice. You did.

midday_events.title: Is This Real?
midday_events.body: A collector slides a boxed game across the counter. 'Can you tell me if this is legitimate?' You're not a grader, but you have eyes.

midday_events.title: Trade-In Flood
midday_events.body: A regular shows up with three plastic bins of used cartridges hoping to trade in. Vic Harlow is on the floor handling intake; you watch him sort the pile and quote prices.

midday_events.title: Someone Cleaned Out an Attic
midday_events.body: A tired-looking parent drops a cardboard box on the counter. Thirty-odd cartridges, no cases. She wants $20 for the lot.

midday_events.title: Demo Unit Down
midday_events.body: The demo console on the end cap has a bad cartridge connector. Customers keep walking away frustrated.

midday_events.title: Suspicious Hold Slip
midday_events.body: A hold slip in the back has yesterday's date and no customer name. The item is one of the high-value sealed boxes. Vic isn't on the floor right now.

choices.label: Put the item back on the shelf

midday_events.title: Regular Wants a Pick
midday_events.body: A regular asks what you'd recommend in the under-$15 range. He trusts your taste.

choices.label: Point him to a confident pick

midday_events.title: Informal Pre-Order
midday_events.body: A regular asks if you'd take $10 down on a title he's sure will surface used in two weeks. You don't have a formal preorder system.

midday_events.title: Warranty Complaint
midday_events.body: A customer returns with a console that died after a week. He doesn't have a receipt but swears he bought it here.

midday_events.title: Shoplifter Spotted
midday_events.body: You catch a teenager slipping a small game into his backpack. He freezes when you make eye contact.

choices.label: Let him put it back quietly
Look away
choices.consequence: Item walks out, hidden tally noted

midday_events.title: Local Paper Stops By
midday_events.body: A reporter from the regional paper pokes her head in. 'Mind if I write a short feature on the store?'

choices.label: Hand her a flyer and get back to work

midday_events.title: Supplier Calls Late
midday_events.body: A supplier calls mid-shift: tomorrow's restock will be a day late. He asks if you want to pay rush fees to keep the original date.

midday_events.title: Manager Quick Ask
midday_events.body: Vic comes by the register. 'Cover the back office for ten minutes? I need to take a call.' The floor will be unattended.

midday_events.title: Launch Day Reservation Conflict
midday_events.body: The hold list for the VecForce HD launch unit names a customer who hasn't shown. A walk-in is at the counter offering full price plus a $50 tip. Vic is in a meeting upstairs.

choices.label: Set the unit aside for later
choices.consequence: Hidden tally noted, scapegoat risk rises
